title: rumex acetosella subsp. angiocarpus
intro: Rumex acetosella subsp. angiocarpus, also known as sheep's sorrel, is a subspecies of the sorrel plant that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout due to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of sheep's sorrel include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Sheep's sorrel can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
